title: Fix undefined CSS custom properties in Tailwind config
notes: CRITICAL VISUAL BUG: tailwind.config.ts defines custom colors referencing CSS variables: --accent-warm, --accent-deep, --ink, --ink-soft. But index.css :root only defines --background, --foreground, --card, --popover, --accent, --surface, --primary, --secondary, --muted, --destructive, --border, --input, --ring, --radius. Missing variables mean any Tailwind class using them (e.g. bg-accent-warm in location-search.tsx line 171) resolves to transparent/inherit. Fix: Either add the missing CSS variables to index.css or remove them from Tailwind config.

title: Fix double-border visual bug in chart section
notes: CRITICAL VISUAL BUG: dashboard.tsx wraps WeatherChart in a Frame component (inline-style bordered box), but WeatherChart/WeatherChartInner renders its own Tailwind Card with rounded-2xl border shadow-sm. Result: two nested borders, a rounded card floating inside a square-edged frame, shadow rendering against flat background. Looks broken. Fix: Remove the Frame wrapper from ChartSection or remove the Card wrapper from WeatherChartInner so only one container renders.

title: Remove dead component files and consolidate formatters
notes: Three component files are completely dead code (~393 lines): components/summary-cards.tsx, components/observation-table.tsx, components/location-search.tsx. None are imported anywhere. The live dashboard.tsx implements equivalent functionality inline. Also in lib/weather.ts: formatTemperature, formatPrecipitation, formatDate, formatTimestamp, formatDateRange, buildLocationKey, coordinateLabel, DashboardData type are only used by dead components or not at all. dashboard.tsx duplicates formatting with its own fmtTemp, fmtPrecip, fmtDate, fmtDateYear. Fix: Delete dead component files and their unused exports from weather.ts, then consolidate formatters so dashboard.tsx uses shared helpers.

title: Fix misleading temperature colors in hero section
notes: MAJOR UX ISSUE: dashboard.tsx HeroSection always colors max temperature in P.hot (#B0563A warm orange-red) and min temperature in P.cold (#3F6B6B cold teal-blue), regardless of actual values. A 12C max in winter shows as 'hot' and a 25C min in desert shows as 'cold'. The color coding implies anomaly/abnormality but is purely positional. Fix: Either color based on actual temperature value using the existing tempColor() helper, or base color on delta from historical average (which is already computed as deltaH/deltaL but not wired to display colors).

title: Handle leap-year Feb 29 edge case in today vs historical
notes: MAJOR EDGE CASE: lib/weather.ts:206-235 computeTodayVsHistorical filters same-day history by monthDay (e.g. '02-29'). On Feb 29 of a leap year, if no previous Feb 29 observations exist (likely for stations with <4 years data), sameDay is empty and the function returns null. The dashboard then silently hides the entire HeroSection. User sees nothing instead of a helpful message. Fix: Fall back to Feb 28 data when no Feb 29 match exists in a leap year, or show a 'no historical comparison available' message.

title: Fix StrictMode double-fetch on initial load
notes: MAJOR BUG: hooks/use-weather.ts:221-223 has useEffect(() => { loadLocations() }, [loadLocations]). main.tsx wraps App in StrictMode, which intentionally double-invokes effects in development. This causes loadLocations() to run twice on mount. The 'seed default location' branch can fire twice, creating duplicate entries and dispatching two concurrent Open-Meteo API requests. Fix: Use a ref to track whether initialization has already run, so the second StrictMode invocation is a no-op.

title: Fix race condition and stale closure in location search
notes: MAJOR BUG in dead component: components/location-search.tsx:38-65 has a debounced search effect with two problems: (1) onSearch is a new function reference whenever state.locations changes (via useCallback dependency), causing the effect to re-run on every location add/remove even if query hasn't changed. (2) The async callback can call setResults/setSearching/setError after component unmounts, causing React warnings. Fix: Store onSearch in a ref to avoid dependency churn, and use an abort flag or AbortController to cancel in-flight searches on unmount. Note: this component is currently dead code, but if reactivated this bug must be fixed.

title: Add delete and refresh controls to station rail
notes: MAJOR UX GAP: dashboard.tsx:1531-1532 explicitly discards deleteLocation and refreshCurrentLocation with void expressions. The StationsRail component receives onSearch, onSelect, onCache but has no onDelete or onRefresh props. Users can add stations but never remove them from the dashboard UI, nor refresh stale data. The only way to remove a location is to clear IndexedDB manually. Compare with the dead location-search.tsx which supports both. Fix: Add delete button (trash icon) and refresh button (spin icon) to each station rail item, wire them to the existing actions.

title: Remove unnecessary React.lazy() for WeatherChartInner
notes: MINOR: components/weather-chart.tsx:6-10 uses React.lazy() to load WeatherChartInner, which is a tiny 206-line component. The actual heavy dependency is echarts-for-react (already bundled). The lazy boundary adds code-splitting overhead, an unnecessary Suspense fallback, and a loading flash. Since ECharts is bundled anyway, there's no meaningful bundle-size savings. Fix: Remove lazy/Suspense and import WeatherChartInner directly.

title: Add retry button to error banner
notes: MINOR UX: dashboard.tsx:1603-1613 shows an error banner when errors occur, but provides no 'Retry' button. Users must reload the page or switch locations to recover from transient failures (e.g. network timeout). Fix: Add a retry button to the error banner that calls the appropriate action (e.g. refreshCurrentLocation() or selectLocation()).

title: Fix inconsistent date boundary operators in range filters
notes: MINOR INCONSISTENCY: lib/weather.ts uses different boundary operators for similar date-range filtering: computeRangeAgg uses '>' (exclusive lower bound), computeCalendarYear uses '>=' (inclusive lower bound), computeAnomalies uses '>' (exclusive). All three do 'N days back from last observation' filtering but the operators differ, making the code brittle and confusing. Fix: Standardize on one convention (recommend '>=' for inclusive) and document it in a comment.
